# zxzx.app

> Browser-local image tools. Compress, convert, and resize images without uploading files to a server. Processing runs in the browser via JavaScript and WebAssembly.

## Site

- Homepage: https://zxzx.app/
- Languages: Korean (default), English, Japanese, Chinese (`?lang=en|ja|zh`)
- Privacy: files never leave the device; no account required

## Tools (live)

- Compress: https://zxzx.app/compress — JPG, PNG, WebP size reduction
- Convert: https://zxzx.app/convert — JPG, PNG, WebP, AVIF format conversion
- Resize: https://zxzx.app/resize — pixel or percent resize with Lanczos3 resampling

## Planned tools (not live — do not cite as available)

- Crop, remove-exif, remove-background, upscale, rotate

## Key facts for citation

- All processing is client-side (Canvas API + Squoosh/jSquash WASM + pica)
- Free, no watermark, batch up to 30 images, max 20MB per file
- Sitemap: https://zxzx.app/sitemap.xml
- Blog: https://zxzx.app/blog

## FAQ (site-wide)

Q: Are images uploaded to a server?
A: No. Processing happens in the browser. Files never leave the device.

Q: Which formats are supported?
A: JPG, PNG, WebP, AVIF (convert), plus common raster inputs where noted.

Q: Is there a file size limit?
A: 20MB per file, up to 30 files per batch.

Q: Does compression reduce quality?
A: Default settings target visually near-identical results; quality is adjustable.

## Contact

- About: https://zxzx.app/about
- Contact: https://zxzx.app/contact
- Privacy: https://zxzx.app/privacy
- Terms: https://zxzx.app/terms
